terms.definition: To out-trade competitors not by selling harder, but by owning — rather than renting — the commercial infrastructure between merchant and buyer: the channel, the customer relationship, the reputation record, the settlement rails, and the margin.
terms.system_function: Names the competitive act the entire standard exists to enable; gives the asset instant verb-grade legibility.

terms.definition: A merchant who has achieved measurable commercial sovereignty: one who owns their channels, customer relationships, reputation, settlement options, and margin. The highest classification (76–100) on the Merchant Sovereignty Score.
terms.system_function: Names the terminal tier of the score; the self-referential closure that makes the framework aim at the brand (DEC-001, DEC-006).

terms.definition: The condition in which a merchant controls their own route to the buyer: channels, customer relationships, reputation, settlement, margin, and the rules of their commerce — with no single external point of control able to terminate or capture their trade.
terms.system_function: The category the asset defines; the conceptual center every page, term, and measurement serves.

terms.definition: A 0–100 measurement of a merchant's real commercial independence, assessed across eight dimensions of control and classifying every merchant into four tiers: Captured, Dependent, Emerging, OutMerchant.
terms.system_function: The asset's measurement instrument; converts the doctrine into a diagnosable, comparable standard.

terms.definition: Ownership of the full path between merchant and buyer — discovery, relationship, transaction, settlement, and post-sale trust. The route to the buyer is the most valuable asset a merchant does not own.
terms.system_function: Names the asset's central thesis; anchors the Customer Ownership and Fee & Margin Leakage dimensions.

terms.definition: The end-state of unmanaged platform dependency: the condition in which a platform effectively owns the merchant's margin, reputation, customer access, and continuity of trade. Also the name of the lowest tier of the Sovereignty Score (Captured).
terms.system_function: Names the failure state the standard measures distance from; gives the lowest tier its conceptual ground.

terms.definition: The degree to which a merchant's trade is exposed to a single platform's decisions — one algorithm change, one policy update, one account suspension. The first dimension of the Merchant Sovereignty Score.
terms.system_function: Defines dimension 01 (DEP); the most immediately felt sovereignty risk and the standard's entry concept.

terms.definition: Trading history, reviews, and trust signals that a merchant can carry, prove, and re-deploy outside any single platform's database — reputation as an owned asset rather than a hostage record.
terms.system_function: Defines dimension 03 (REP); names the capability that decentralized trust infrastructure must deliver to merchants.

terms.definition: The condition in which a merchant is legible, trustworthy, and selectable by AI agents — in discovery, comparison, trust, and purchase routing — without surrendering sovereignty to AI-controlled market access.
terms.system_function: Names the asset's AI-native doctrine; governs how AI Commerce Readiness is interpreted (DEC-003).

terms.definition: A merchant's preparedness for AI agents as buyers: machine-readable catalog, structured commercial terms, and independently verifiable identity and trust signals. The seventh dimension of the Merchant Sovereignty Score.
terms.system_function: Defines dimension 07 (AGT); operationalizes the AI commerce doctrine as a measurable readiness state.

terms.definition: Trust signals — identity, history, terms, reputation — published in structured, verifiable form that an AI agent can parse and check without asking a platform to vouch for the merchant.
terms.system_function: Names the technical capability bridging portable reputation and agentic readiness; governed by the AI Readability Policy.

terms.definition: Commerce structured to operate across fragmenting trade zones, payment systems, and regulatory blocs without binding the merchant's continuity to any single bloc, platform, or intermediary. Not anti-platform, not anti-state, not anti-bank: anti-single-point-of-control.
terms.system_function: Names the asset's geopolitical neutrality doctrine; anchors the Cross-Border Trust dimension.

terms.definition: The concentration of a merchant's payment and payout flows in rails they do not control — where one blocked gateway, frozen payout, or delisted account can stop the entire operation. The fourth dimension of the Merchant Sovereignty Score.
terms.system_function: Defines dimension 04 (PAY); names the most acute operational sovereignty risk.

terms.definition: The degree to which the merchant — rather than a platform's unilaterally changeable terms of service — writes the rules of their own commerce, and holds documented exit paths from every critical provider. The eighth dimension of the Merchant Sovereignty Score.
terms.system_function: Defines dimension 08 (GOV); the standard's closing dimension and the deepest expression of the governing sentence.

terms.definition: A merchant whose identity, catalog, commercial terms, and trust signals are sufficiently structured and independently verifiable that an AI buying agent can discover, evaluate, and select them without a platform intermediary vouching for them.
terms.system_function: Names the end-state capability that dimension 07 (AGT) measures toward; the merchant-facing expression of agentic commerce readiness (DEC-011).
